title: thymus serpyllum var. longicaulis
intro: Thymus serpyllum var. longicaulis, also known as creeping thyme or wild thyme, is a low-growing, flowering plant belonging to the mint family. It has been used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which can help to combat infections and promote wound healing. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, improving respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, and possessing anti-inflammatory properties. Thymus serpyllum var. longicaulis can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and well-being.
